review.summary: The executor is well-designed and fail-closed, but the quality gate references reviews/2026-07-18-executor-pr1-claude-review.json (json.tool) which is not created by this diff, so the CI script and its tests would fail. A recheck TOCTOU gap exists: recheck re-verifies remote state but the model already ran; state can change between prepare and publish only partially mitigated. verify_run_budget uses '>' not '>=' allowing an off-by-one (total_limit+1 runs pass since current run is counted). These block approval.

findings.finding: quality-gate.sh runs `python3 -m json.tool reviews/2026-07-18-executor-pr1-claude-review.json` but this diff only adds reviews/2026-07-18-executor-pr1-codex-adjudication.md; the JSON file is absent from the diff, so CI fail-closed tests error at that line. The security-checks job cannot pass on this Head as presented.

verify_run_budget compares len(current_runs) > total_limit and pilot count > pilot_limit. The dispatching run itself is included in the ledger, so exactly total_limit+1 dispatched runs (or pilot_limit+1) are permitted before denial, exceeding the stated maximum_claude_reviews_total=6 / per_pilot=2 caps by one call.

Workflow-level budget enforcement relies on GitHub Actions run listing eventual consistency. A concurrent second dispatch (concurrency group serializes review job, but paused-gate/prepare of a second run may race) could both read a stale run count. No atomic reservation binds the run count to prevent double-spend of a Claude call, unlike the local bridge's file lock.

append_github_env writes the multiline prompt into GITHUB_ENV; the delimiter is derived from sha256(value), but if evidence content contained that exact 64-hex string prefixed by TREEXCHANGE_ it is rejected only by substring check. Acceptable, but env-var prompt injection surface remains larger than passing via file input.

No tools granted to Claude in either lane; API-key/alternate-provider env rejected; secrets scanned; duplicate-key JSON forced to CHANGES_REQUESTED; reruns denied at attempt>1; fork PRs and stale Head rejected.

verification: Local bridge and workflow pass no file/shell/web/MCP tools to Claude and reject disallowed auth env vars.
validate_result enforces APPROVE cannot carry open P0/P1/P2 and strips injected structured-field markers.
reserve_attempt uses an OS file lock; concurrency test asserts exactly one of eight duplicate reservations succeeds.
Workflow guards both jobs with run_attempt==1 and ref main; rerun and fork PRs are denied.
validate_config pins action SHAs, model policy, limits, and forbidden effects against drift.
requirement_coverage: Credential exposure: tokens confined to environment secrets; logs suppress structured output; secret patterns scanned.
Budget bypass: caps enforced via ledger and run-count, though off-by-one and race gaps remain (see findings).
Fail-closed: paused config, missing/stale/duplicate state, and non-zero Claude exit all deny.
Unsafe automation: no merge/push/deploy; publish only posts a validated comment via read-scoped token.
residual_risk: Administrator can alter branch/environment protection; mitigated only by user-approved SHA pinning, not cryptographically.
Public dispatch metadata exposes PR/Head/request IDs unless public_dispatch_metadata_accepted is explicitly set.
Off-by-one budget and Actions-listing race allow at most one extra Claude call under contention.
